title: alchemilla geranioides var. uhligii
intro: Alchemilla geranioides var. uhligii, also known as the Lady's Mantle, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its rosettes of rounded, toothed leaves and white or greenish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The medicinal properties of Lady's Mantle have been linked to various health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding, improving digestion, and supporting liver function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the Lady's Mantle can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
